description: This figure comprises two line-graph subplots presenting the room-temperature mechanical properties of as-cast Fe–10Al–xMn–1.0C alloy sheet castings, labeled alloys A through H (corresponding to 5–40 mass% Mn). Subplot (a) plots ultimate tensile strength (UTS, solid diamonds) and yield strength (YS, solid squares) in MPa versus alloy designation, while subplot (b) plots elongation (EL, solid diamonds) in percent and hardness (solid squares) in HRC versus alloy designation. Visually, both UTS and YS in (a) rise from alloy A to a maximum at alloy C (15 mass% Mn) and then progressively decline toward alloy H; the caption reports peak values of 887 MPa for UTS and 824 MPa for YS at this composition. Hardness in (b) follows a similar non-monotonic trend, reaching a maximum near alloy C (approximately HRC 43) before decreasing to about HRC 31 in alloy H. Conversely, elongation increases monotonically from roughly 16% in alloy A to about 29% in alloy H. These opposing trends demonstrate that increasing Mn content initially strengthens the duplex austenite-plus-ferrite microstructure via carbide precipitation and solid-solution effects, but beyond 15 mass% Mn the declining α/D03 phase fraction and associated coarse κ″ carbide distribution reduce strength and hardness while markedly improving ductility.
